mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from residential investment to commercial capital expenditure, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the risk-free rate and discounting future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.2 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reduces recession risk premiums and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rates against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 in a single month, it signals robust economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer credit expands while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent creates abundant liquidity that flows into equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, rising unemployment acts as a lagging indicator of weakening demand, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises concurrently over a 3-month window.
